evals.prompt: Write a Pi extension that blocks any bash command containing `curl` unless the user confirms, and logs all blocked commands to a file at ~/.pi/agent/blocked-commands.log
evals.expected_output: A TypeScript extension file that hooks into tool_call events, checks for curl in bash commands, prompts via ctx.ui.select, and appends blocked commands to a log file. Should check ctx.hasUI and handle non-interactive mode.
assertions: Uses correct ExtensionAPI import from @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent
Hooks into tool_call event (the correct event for intercepting tool calls)
Checks event.toolName === 'bash' before inspecting command
Tests for curl in the command string
Checks ctx.hasUI before calling interactive UI methods
Uses ctx.ui.select or ctx.ui.confirm for user confirmation
Returns { block: true, reason: '...' } to prevent execution
Logs blocked commands to ~/.pi/agent/blocked-commands.log
Uses correct export default function (pi: ExtensionAPI) signature

evals.prompt: I want to set up Pi to use Ollama with llama3.2 locally, with auto-compaction disabled and a custom session directory at ~/pi-sessions. Walk me through the configuration.
evals.expected_output: Instructions covering models.json with Ollama provider config (baseUrl localhost:11434/v1, api openai-completions, dummy apiKey, model id llama3.2), settings.json with compaction.enabled=false and sessionDir pointing to ~/pi-sessions.
assertions: Provides models.json with Ollama baseUrl containing localhost:11434
Uses api type openai-completions (correct for Ollama)
Includes model entry with id llama3.2
Provides settings.json with compaction.enabled set to false
Sets sessionDir to ~/pi-sessions
Correct file paths: ~/.pi/agent/models.json and ~/.pi/agent/settings.json

evals.prompt: Create a Pi skill that helps with database migrations — it should know about Prisma, Drizzle, and raw SQL approaches. Give me the full SKILL.md and any reference files.
evals.expected_output: A SKILL.md with proper frontmatter (name, description), a main body routing to reference files, and separate reference docs for Prisma, Drizzle, and raw SQL migration patterns.
assertions: SKILL.md has valid YAML frontmatter with name and description fields
Name follows Pi convention: lowercase, hyphens only, max 64 chars
Description mentions trigger contexts (migrations, Prisma, Drizzle, SQL)
Includes separate reference files for Prisma, Drizzle, and raw SQL
SKILL.md uses relative paths to reference files
Body routes to the correct reference doc based on the user's framework choice
